extraction.one_line_summary: HINDLEY Jai won Blockhouse after a six-rider sprint finish, LÓPEZ Juan Pedro held pink by just 12 seconds after trailing by 1:46.
tactical_patterns: PORTE Richie isolated everyone by setting tempo as last domestique, forcing GC favorites into stalemate rather than cooperation.
YATES Simon abandoned GC contention with 12km remaining on Blockhouse despite strapped knee, revealing vulnerability in week one crashes.
LÓPEZ Juan Pedro lost 1:46 but retained pink by 12s, proving early race jersey defense thresholds are tighter than expected.

rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: Won uphill sprint after Blockhouse despite losing positioning final corner, suggesting superior kick even when disadvantaged.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Dropped 12km from summit of first major GC test with knee problems from Etna crash, effectively out of podium contention.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: Survived 1:46 loss to hold pink by 12s, showing unexpected resilience for 24-year-old non-GC specialist on hardest finish.
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: Last domestique standing for any team on Blockhouse, tempo ride prevented cooperation among six-man GC group.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Part of six-man group but failed to make decisive move despite LANDA Mikel and ALMEIDA João present.
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: negative: Early crash required repairs, finished outside top-six group on decisive GC stage.
